Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 058. Transporting Basalt Slabs (ABL 1049)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na LUGAL EN-ia]

(1) [To the king, my lord: your servant Ṭab-šar-Aš]šur. [Good health] to the king, my lord!

o 22

[ARAD-ka mDÙG—IM—aš]-⸢šur⸣

o 33

[lu DI-mu a]-⸢na LUGAL EN⸣-ia

o 44

[TAv É.GAL] i-sa-par-u-ni

(4) They wrote to me from the Palace: "Let them cut out 150 basalt steps, load them up and bring them to Nineveh."

o 55

ina UGU-ḫi-ia ma-a 01 me 50

o 66

e-bir-tú ša NA₄.AD.BAR

o 77

lib-tu-qu li-in-tu-ḫu-ni

o 88

a-na URU.NINA lu-bi-lu-ni

o 99

an-nu-rig ab-ta-taq

(9) I have now cut and loaded them, [and am com]ing to Nineveh; [let the king, my lord, send word] whether I should bring them to the ba[throom] of the god itself [or ......

o 1010

⸢at⸣-ta-at-ḫa a-na URU.NINA

o 1111

[al-la]-⸢ka⸣ šúm-mu a-na

o 1212

[É—ra]-⸢ma⸣-ki ša DINGIR šú-u

o 1313

[x x la]-⸢an⸣-tu-ḫu [x x x]

Bottom
b.e. 14b.e. 14

[x x x x]+⸢x x⸣+[x x x]

one line destroyed

(Rest destroyed)

Reverse
rr uninscribed

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334699/.
